title: Fix
text:
---
title: Fixing node issues
description: Fixing node issues
---

:::caution
This page contains administrative documentation intended for cluster administrators and operators. This content may not be relevant for regular users
:::

import { Steps } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

## Once you find an issue on the node that can affect any user job

<Steps>

    1. Create an issue in GitLab describing the problem and taint the node with the issue number

        ```
        kubectl taint node-1 nautilus.io/issue=issue_number:NoSchedule
        ```

        <Steps>

        1. If you're using the [zsh-scripts.sh](https://gitlab.nrp-nautilus.io/prp/nautilus-ansible/-/blob/master/zsh-scripts.sh) script from Ansible repo, use the `node_maint` function to automagically create the issue and taint the node with it

        </Steps>

    1. Drain the node. **Check all pods except the system ones are gone**

    1. Check that all storage volumes are successfully unmounted from the node (both `lsblk` devices and k8s volumeattachments)

    1. Check **in linstor controller pod** that [no linstor volumes exist on the node (even diskless ones)](/documentation/admindocs/storage/linstor/#cleaning-up-a-node)

    1. Delete the spegel pod - it's not tolerating the `nautilus.io/issue` taint and won't restart. If you need containerd to work, change the config:

        ```
        sudo -s
        sed -i '/^[[:space:]]*config_path = "\/etc\/containerd\/certs\.d"$/d' /etc/containerd/config.toml
        rm -rf /etc/containerd/certs.d
        systemctl restart containerd
        ```

    1. Fix the issue

</Steps>

## When the issue is fixed

<Steps>

    1. [Verify the node connectivity](/documentation/admindocs/fiona/install/#verifying-the-connectivity)

    2. If spegel was diabled, put back the config for it and restart containerd:
